title: Malaga Jet Ski Rental: Costs, Licences and Top Spots for 2026
html:
If you are planning a trip to Malaga, renting a jet ski offers an exhilarating way to explore the Costa del Sol. Depart from the modern Marina del Este or the buzzing Muelle Uno harbour, and expect to pay between EUR 80 and EUR 150 for a half-hour session, depending on the craft and season. BoatHire24 is the easiest way to compare verified boat listings across dozens of local operators.

How much does it cost to rent a jet ski in Malaga?

Prices start around EUR 80 for a shared 30-minute ride and rise to EUR 300 for a private hour-long charter. Most hire companies operate from the main marinas in Benalmádena, Torremolinos and the port of Malaga itself. Peak season rates apply from June to September, when demand is highest. For a complete overview of available vessels, you can compare verified boat listings directly on the platform.

Where to set off in Malaga

The most popular launch points include Puerto Deportivo de Benalmádena, Marina del Este, Muelle Uno in the city centre, and the smaller harbour at Fuengirola. Each offers direct access to the calm waters of the Alboran Sea and clear views of the coast. Many operators also provide guided tours to the nearby Cabo de Gata natural park or the chic Malaga marina.

Which boat to rent

Jet skis vary widely in power and comfort. Here are four common types you will find for hire in Malaga.

  1. Standard two-seater jet ski, best for a quick solo blast, seats 2, from EUR 80 for 30 minutes.
  2. Three-seater touring model, ideal for couples or a parent and child, seats 3, from EUR 120 per half hour.
  3. Luxury performance jet ski, higher horsepower and more comfortable seats, seats 2, 3, from EUR 200 per hour.
  4. Large family-sized jet ski, stable and easy to handle, seats 4, from EUR 250 per hour.

One excellent choice is the Mefasa 90 for hire, a well-maintained vessel perfect for exploring the Malaga coastline.

Do you need a licence to rent a jet ski in Malaga?

Spain requires a valid boat licence for jet skis over a certain horsepower, but short-term rental operators often include a quick safety briefing that covers basic navigation. Legally, you need the Spanish 'licencia de navegación' or an equivalent International Certificate of Competence (ICC). Many hire companies in Malaga will confirm exact requirements when you book. For a thorough guide to the booking process and local rules, consult the booking FAQ on BoatHire24.

Tip: book your jet ski for early morning or late afternoon in July and August to avoid the strong Levante wind that can make the sea choppy after midday.

Cost breakdown for jet ski rental in Malaga

  • Shared guided tour (30 min): EUR 80, 100 per person
  • Small open boat for half day (4 hours, up to 6 guests): from EUR 120
  • Catamaran full-day charter (8 hours, up to 12 guests): from EUR 800
  • Crewed yacht day trip (8 hours, up to 10 guests): from EUR 1,500

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 80, EUR 300
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 30 minutes to 8 hours
  • Maximum guests: 2, 4 (jet ski) or up to 12 (larger boats)
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about jet ski rental in Malaga

Can I rent a jet ski without a licence in Malaga?

Yes, many operators offer a 30-minute or one-hour rental after a brief safety briefing, but you must carry some form of identification and proof of age.

What is the best time of year to rent a jet ski in Malaga?

The best conditions are from May to October, when the sea is calm and water temperatures are pleasant.

How far can I travel on a rented jet ski in Malaga?

Most tours stay within 5 nautical miles of the coast, with popular stops at the Punta de la Baja cliffs or the Isla de Alborán (if allowed by the operator).

Are there any age restrictions for renting a jet ski in Malaga?

Renters must be at least 18 years old, and those under 18 can ride only as passengers with a signed parental waiver.
